title: Salesblazers: A Path to Success for Small Business Owners
author: Caylin White, Niti Praveen

sections.heading: Key Takeaways
sections.text: Embracing a Salesblazer mindset means focusing on growth and using technology to serve the business.Hear advice from Niti Praveen, CEO and Salesblazer, on community, business, and AI.Operationalizing early and investing in agentic tools, like Agentforce 360, can help small teams compete with larger organizations. How do you become a Salesblazer for your small business? For many founders, you feel you are called by sales, the strategy behind it, and the tools that make it happen. So find a community that supports that. SalesblazerNiti Praveendiscovered that success isn’t just about having a ‘cool tool’; it’s about the strategy behind the execution. In this interview, she shares how she transitioned from seeking help to building a global network for women in sales.

sections.heading: What’s a Salesblazer for small business?
sections.text: ASalesblazer for small businessis a modern, successful, and empowered professional who views sales as a path to innovation. This role is more than just a user of technology — it’s a mindset centered on business, sales and using the right tools. For Salesforce, it’s about understanding the customer and your team — and connecting with them. A Salesblazer trusts incustomer relationship management (CRM)to drive smarter decision-making and to scale revenue, breaking out of the small business mindset. Forsmall and medium business (SMB)owners, this means shifting from tool-centric conversations to a bigger picture where technology serves the business needs.

sections.heading: The power of community networking
sections.text: The Salesblazer community provides critical support for SMB leaders who need efficient, integrated tools, expertise and know-how. By joining specialized groups, like the “Salesblazin’ Women” Slack channel, members can access expert advice and huddle together to celebrate wins. Networking and learning: Connect with peers who share similar challenges to find out what tactics are working now.Resource access: UseTrailhead, the free learning platform, to access webinars and success stories specifically for startups.Visibility and advocacy: Participate in Ask Me Anything (AMA) sessions to share job opportunities and professional best practices.

sections.heading: Real-world inspiration: A Salesblazer success story
sections.text: Niti Praveen, a visionary entrepreneur and a defining voice in the next generation of tech leadership, sat down with us to talk about Saleblazers. As the Founder and CEO ofKnowCloudAI, Niti is at the forefront of theartificial intelligence (AI)revolution, guiding organizations through the complexities of the digital frontier with a fresh, dynamic perspective. Niti’s influence extends deep into the global tech ecosystem through her prestigious role on theSalesforce Community Advisory Boardand her leadership as a Slack Community Chapter Leader. Recognized as a “Trailblazer” in the truest sense, she embodies the spirit of innovation, proving that community is a powerful catalyst for change in the era of AI. Whether she’s building community or scaling AI-driven solutions, Niti is committed to shaping a future where technology is both cutting-edge and community-focused. Niti’s origin story began with a desire to create a space where strategy and execution were not separate. Let’s find out her story to becoming a Salesblazer:

sections.heading: Interview time: How did you become a Salesblazer for small business?
sections.text: Niti: My background has been rooted in the fast-paced world of sales, specifically within the SMB and growth sectors. I started in software engineering but I quickly realized I wanted to build a niche. I noticed companies invested in powerful technology but didn’t always get the value because the approach was too tool-centric. My journey in the Salesforce ecosystem began around 2015, following my Masters in Software Engineering. I held prominent roles, including marketing at Michelin, before moving on to work with other major companies in waste management. This experience across different facets of sales technology and large organizations ultimately fueled my decision to found my own consulting firm. I have been in the Salesforce space for around 10 years now, and being a core part of the engineering team at different firms, I realized a huge gap. I noticed that companies invested in powerful technology without always getting the right value out of it, because the approach was always tool-centric rather than business-first. Even back then, working in a technical role, my mind kept figuring out solutions for the business and functional issues. I started my own firm to create a space where technology served the business and where learning came from experiences and communities. And that’s where I help companies with tools that make their lives easier, everyday.

sections.heading: What is the Salesblazin’ Women’s group all about?
sections.text: Niti: It’s more than just a channel; it’s the very heart of our ecosystem. We have knowledge drops where we share what’s working now, from business tactics to how we useartificial intelligence (AI)to recommendations on tools. Women from all over the globe come to find experts, or be an expert — that’s the beautiful side of it. Both win. We focus on professional presence, like managing calendars while traveling, and we celebrate each other’s wins. We always take time to celebrate the wins across the group, that’s the most important.

sections.heading: What business advice do you have for ‌entrepreneurs out there?
sections.text: To succeed today, Niti recommends that every small business owner stop serving people and start solvingwiththem. This community-first strategy helps leaders identify a niche where they can grow something new. Niti: First, build on a community-first strategy — stop selling to people and start solving with them. Second, operationalize in your early stages because many SMBs think they aren’t big enough for deep data analysis, which is a mistake. Finally, invest in AI. I useAgentforce 360to qualify leads and schedule meetings, which provides digital labor so my team can focus on higher tasks. Build on a community-first strategy: Look for knowledge, and connect with other CEOs — stop serving people and start solving with them.Operationalize early: Don’t wait until you’re a large company to perform deep data analysis or fix process gaps.Invest in AI: Start with agentic tools like Salesforce’s Sales Agent and Agentforce 360 to automate lead scoring and meeting scheduling. Pro Tip:When using AI, don’t just read the first line of an output — dive into the details to understand the “why” behind the data. raw_html:
Key Takeaways

  • Embracing a Salesblazer mindset means focusing on growth and using technology to serve the business.
  • Hear advice from Niti Praveen, CEO and Salesblazer, on community, business, and AI.
  • Operationalizing early and investing in agentic tools, like Agentforce 360, can help small teams compete with larger organizations.
This summary was created with AI and reviewed by an editor.

How do you become a Salesblazer for your small business? For many founders, you feel you are called by sales, the strategy behind it, and the tools that make it happen. So find a community that supports that. 

Salesblazer Niti Praveen discovered that success isn’t just about having a ‘cool tool’; it’s about the strategy behind the execution. In this interview, she shares how she transitioned from seeking help to building a global network for women in sales.

What’s a Salesblazer for small business?

A Salesblazer for small business is a modern, successful, and empowered professional who views sales as a path to innovation. This role is more than just a user of technology — it’s a mindset centered on business, sales and using the right tools. For Salesforce, it’s about understanding the customer and your team — and connecting with them. 

A Salesblazer trusts in customer relationship management (CRM) to drive smarter decision-making and to scale revenue, breaking out of the small business mindset. For small and medium business (SMB) owners, this means shifting from tool-centric conversations to a bigger picture where technology serves the business needs.

Join millions in the growing Salesforce ecosystem

Trailblazers are free to reinvent themselves, free to dream bigger.

Be a Trailblazer

The power of community networking

The Salesblazer community provides critical support for SMB leaders who need efficient, integrated tools, expertise and know-how. By joining specialized groups, like the “Salesblazin’ Women” Slack channel, members can access expert advice and huddle together to celebrate wins.

Connecting with peers

  • Networking and learning: Connect with peers who share similar challenges to find out what tactics are working now.
  • Resource access: Use Trailhead, the free learning platform, to access webinars and success stories specifically for startups.
  • Visibility and advocacy: Participate in Ask Me Anything (AMA) sessions to share job opportunities and professional best practices.

Real-world inspiration: A Salesblazer success story

Niti Praveen, a visionary entrepreneur and a defining voice in the next generation of tech leadership, sat down with us to talk about Saleblazers. As the Founder and CEO of KnowCloudAI, Niti is at the forefront of the artificial intelligence (AI) revolution, guiding organizations through the complexities of the digital frontier with a fresh, dynamic perspective.

Niti’s influence extends deep into the global tech ecosystem through her prestigious role on the Salesforce Community Advisory Board and her leadership as a Slack Community Chapter Leader. Recognized as a “Trailblazer” in the truest sense, she embodies the spirit of innovation, proving that community is a powerful catalyst for change in the era of AI. Whether she’s building community or scaling AI-driven solutions, Niti is committed to shaping a future where technology is both cutting-edge and community-focused.

Niti’s origin story began with a desire to create a space where strategy and execution were not separate. Let’s find out her story to becoming a Salesblazer: 

Interview time: How did you become a Salesblazer for small business?

Niti: My background has been rooted in the fast-paced world of sales, specifically within the SMB and growth sectors. I started in software engineering but I quickly realized I wanted to build a niche. I noticed companies invested in powerful technology but didn’t always get the value because the approach was too tool-centric. 

My journey in the Salesforce ecosystem began around 2015, following my Masters in Software Engineering. I held prominent roles, including marketing at Michelin, before moving on to work with other major companies in waste management. This experience across different facets of sales technology and large organizations ultimately fueled my decision to found my own consulting firm.

I have been in the Salesforce space for around 10 years now, and being a core part of the engineering team at different firms, I realized a huge gap. I noticed that companies invested in powerful technology without always getting the right value out of it, because the approach was always tool-centric rather than business-first.

Even back then, working in a technical role, my mind kept figuring out solutions for the business and functional issues. I started my own firm to create a space where technology served the business and where learning came from experiences and communities. And that’s where I help companies with tools that make their lives easier, everyday. 

What is the Salesblazin’ Women’s group all about?

Niti: It’s more than just a channel; it’s the very heart of our ecosystem. We have knowledge drops where we share what’s working now, from business tactics to how we use artificial intelligence (AI) to recommendations on tools. 

Women from all over the globe come to find experts, or be an expert — that’s the beautiful side of it. Both win. We focus on professional presence, like managing calendars while traveling, and we celebrate each other’s wins. We always take time to celebrate the wins across the group, that’s the most important. 

Boost team productivity with Slack

Bring together your team, your customers, and your tools to help take your business to the next level with Slack — it’s where business gets done.

Try for free

What business advice do you have for ‌entrepreneurs out there?

To succeed today, Niti recommends that every small business owner stop serving people and start solving with them. This community-first strategy helps leaders identify a niche where they can grow something new.

Niti: First, build on a community-first strategy — stop selling to people and start solving with them. Second, operationalize in your early stages because many SMBs think they aren’t big enough for deep data analysis, which is a mistake. Finally, invest in AI. I use Agentforce 360 to qualify leads and schedule meetings, which provides digital labor so my team can focus on higher tasks.

Niti’s advice: How to modernize your operations

  • Build on a community-first strategy: Look for knowledge, and connect with other CEOs — stop serving people and start solving with them. 
  • Operationalize early: Don’t wait until you’re a large company to perform deep data analysis or fix process gaps.
  • Invest in AI: Start with agentic tools like Salesforce’s Sales Agent and Agentforce 360 to automate lead scoring and meeting scheduling.

Pro Tip: When using AI, don’t just read the first line of an output — dive into the details to understand the “why” behind the data.

More resources on the Saleblazers

  • Salesblazer Community: Join the official community to network, share best practices, and get advice from peers.
  • Trailhead: Grow Your Small Business with Salesforce: Access free, guided learning modules designed specifically for SMB owners and growing teams.
  • Salesforce Small Business Solutions: Explore CRM tools and resources tailored to meet the needs of small and medium businesses.

Practical steps for a Salesblazer for small business

Choosing the right path as a Salesblazer means modernizing your approach to growth. Whether you’re a solopreneur or leading a growing team, the goal is to use digital labor to execute tasks so you can focus on building relationships. By staying connected to a global community, you can ensure your small business stays ahead of innovation.
